programs.name: Maryland Durable Medical Equipment Reuse Program (DME)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free durable medical equipment to those in need by accepting donations, sanitizing, repairing, and redistributing items with high safety standards. Serves Marylanders across ages and abilities to support independent living.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Maryland residents of all ages and abilities needing durable medical equipment.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-operated reuse program for durable medical equipment specific to Maryland, focusing on free redistribution after rigorous safety processing.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free sanitized and repaired durable medical equipment.

programs.name: Community First Choice (CFC)
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services as an alternative to nursing homes for eligible Medicaid recipients. Provides personal care, homemaker services, and other supports to help seniors remain in their communities.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors and disabled individuals needing nursing home level of care but preferring home-based services.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive in-home services tailored to individual needs, potentially saving costs compared to institutional care.

programs.name: Maryland PACE
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings like adult day centers. Includes primary care, hospitalization if needed, prescription drugs, and supportive services to avoid nursing home placement.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible with limited income and assets.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitated coverage for Medicare and Medicaid services.

programs.name: Maryland Medicare Savings Program (MSP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income beneficiaries through QMB, SLMB, and QI categories. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and most out-of-pocket costs; SLMB and QI cover Part B premiums.[3][6]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries with limited income and resources (income limits vary: e.g., QMB up to 100% FPL, SLMB 120%, QI 135%).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full premium/cost-sharing coverage; SLMB: Part B premium (~$174.70/mo in 2024); QI: Part B premium for highest eligible.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to purchase healthy food at approved retailers. Helps low-income households afford nutritious meals.[3][5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (60+), disabled individuals, and households meeting income tests (e.g., gross income ≤130% FPL).
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per household; max for 1 person ~$291/month (2024 rates, adjusted annually).

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help with heating and electric bills, crisis assistance, and furnace repairs. Reduces energy burden for vulnerable households.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households, prioritizing seniors 60+, disabled, families with children under 6; income ≤150% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants $200-$1,000+ depending on fuel type, income, household size, and need.

programs.name: Maryland Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and efficient appliances. Lowers energy costs and improves home safety/comfort.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters, prioritizing seniors, disabled, and families with children; income ≤200% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Free comprehensive weatherization services valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare options, savings programs, and health insurance issues. Helps prevent fraud via Senior Medicare Patrol (SMP).[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Maryland Medicare beneficiaries, especially seniors seeking guidance on coverage and costs.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; potential savings thousands annually through proper enrollment.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors through Area Agencies on Aging. Promotes health and reduces isolation via wellness checks.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, meeting low-income or at-risk guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week; ~$8-10/meal cost covered for eligible (suggested donation for others).

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides information, counseling, training, and respite services to family caregivers. Offers supplemental services like respite to relieve caregiver burden.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+, grandparents raising grandchildren, older relatives caring for children.
programs.benefit_value: Respite up to $32/hour; counseling and training at low/no cost.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid work training at community sites leading to unsubsidized employment. Builds skills for older workers.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ (income ≤125% FPL) unable to find work.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/wk, up to 48 wks/yr); avg $10-15/hr.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services for civil issues like benefits, housing, and consumer problems. Delivered via Maryland Legal Aid and pro bono partners.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal needs.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and similar facilities. Investigates complaints and resolves issues to protect rights.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities and their families in Maryland.

programs.name: Maryland Senior Call Check Program (SCC)
programs.what_it_does: This program provides a daily automated call to verify the well-being of participants at a scheduled time of their convenience. Registration is free for eligible residents.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Maryland residents aged 65 years and older.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific automated daily wellness check calls unique to Maryland, not a standard federal program.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Free daily automated well-being verification calls.

programs.name: Senior Assisted Living Subsidy Program (SALS)
programs.what_it_does: Provides a monthly subsidy to help cover costs for residents of licensed assisted living facilities. Supports services including meals, personal care, and 24-hour supervision for those needing assistance.[1][3][5]
programs.who_its_for: Maryland residents aged 65 or older meeting specific eligibility criteria, such as income and assessment requirements, residing in licensed assisted living facilities.[1][3][5]
programs.whats_unique: Maryland state-funded monthly subsidy specifically for assisted living facility residents, beyond federal Medicaid options and not universally available in other states.[1][3][5]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly subsidy amount varies by eligibility and need; covers assisted living services.

programs.name: Senior Care Services Program
programs.what_it_does: Coordinates community-based in-home services, equipment, medical supplies, personal care, chore services, home-delivered meals, and local transportation. Aims to help seniors maintain independent living and avoid nursing home placement through assessment and case management.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Maryland residents aged 65 or older at risk of nursing facility placement, determined via face-to-face assessment.[1][3]
programs.whats_unique: State-funded program offering coordinated in-home long-term care services tailored to Maryland seniors, exceeding standard federal home care options.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by assessment; includes personal care, meals, transportation, supplies, and equipment.
